content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

July 8, 1976

Mr. Ronald Kaldor
Executive Vice President
California Republican League
47 DeRow Court
Sacramento, CA 95833

Dear Mr. Kaldor:

Re: Opinion Request
No. 76-015

As you know, the Commission has adopted regulations
that are relevant to your opinion request. 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Sections 18215, 18225. Accordingly, the Commission has
instructed the staff to send you this letter in lieu of the
formal opinion that you requested.

Because the California Republican League is a general
political organization that carries on issue discussions and
various volunteer activities as well as activities supporting candidates, it is not an organization formed or existing
primarily for political purposes. It is also not an official
committee of the Republican Party. Consequently, dues received by the Menlo-Atherton chapter of the California Republican League are "contributions" to the extent that the dues
payments are "earmarked" for the making of contributions or
"expenditures. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215(d). If the
club uses the dues to pay for a newsletter and cover costs
of monthly meetings, it is unlikely that any portion of the
dues are "contributions."

Funds received for the purpose of conducting the state
convention for the California Republican League are not
contributions if the funds are used only to cover the costs
of meals, room rental, and other expenses of running the
convention. In this case, the person attending the convention receives full and adequate consideration for the payment
rendered to the club.

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

Mr. Ronald Kaldor
July 8, 1976
Page Two

I hope this information will be of assistance. Please
contact Natalie West, an attorney with the Legal Division of
the Commission staff, if you have any further questions.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:plh

47 DeRow Court
Sacramento, California 95833
January 30, 1976


Mr. Daniel Lowenstein
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Chairman Lowenstein:

This letter is to request a legal opinion regarding the following
facts:

1. The California Republican League (CRL) is a statewide volunteer political organization, the ultimate purpose of which
is the election of Republican candidates to partisan office.

2. The CRL has local chapters located in various parts of the
state which engage in activities affecting partisan and nonpartisan activities.

3. The CRL files as a campaign committee and files appropriate
campaign reports.

Question: (a) Must the Menlo-Atherton chapter of the CRL file
as a campaign committee if it receives over $500.00 in "dues"
and spends over $500.00 only on publications and meetings
informing and educating their members on the methods of electing
candidates from their particular political party?

(b) Does this local unit have to file a campaign committee statement if, in addition to the above, it holds receptions honoring
particular candidates?

(c) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee
if, in addition to the above, it contributes money, volunteers,
or other campaign assistance to particular candidates?

(d) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee
if, in addition to the above, it receives funds and makes expenditures for the specific purpose of conducting an event of the
parent CRL such as a state convention? Assume in this case that
funds received and spent for the convention were both over $500.00.


Mr. Daniel Lowenstein
Page 2
January 30, 1976


In all of the above, assume that the local unit is acting
within the bylaws of the state political organization.

Yours truly,

[signature]

RONALD KALDOR
Executive Vice President
California Republican League

(d) Does this local unit have to file as a campaign committee if, in addition to the above, it receives funds and makes expenditures for the specific purpose of conducting an event of the parent CRL such as a state convention? Assume in this case that funds received and spent for the convention were both over $500.00.


Mr. Daniel Lowenstein    
January 30, 1976

Yours truly,

RONALD KALDOR  
Executive Vice President  
California Republican League
